name: API From Hell
description: A deliberately awkward API for checking how REST clients display bad responses.

endpoints.label: Status - Markdown Documentation
endpoints.documentation: Markdown status text describing the intentionally awkward response set.
endpoints.problem: This is a good response, but it is markdown rather than JSON or XML.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should show the response body as text/markdown and make the content type visible.
endpoints.body:
# Mock API From Hell

An API designed to test and evaluate REST Clients.

- Do the REST Clients help spot the errors?
- Are error messages readable and helpful?
- Do the REST Clients flag errors automatically?

API Should have a Swagger file to make it easy to import into clients.

User should be able to execute requests quickly and spot obvious issues.

## Errors covered

- No Error
   - [x] good endpoints to cover main content types to check REST Client can render and handle them
- Additional Content Formats
   - [x] Plain text
   - [x] HTML
   - [x] CSV
   - [x] YAML
   - [x] Form URL encoded
   - [x] Octet stream
- Missing Content-Type
   - [x] JSON body without Content-Type
   - [x] XML body without Content-Type
   - [x] HTML body without Content-Type
- API Returns malformed JSON
   - [x] Missing final collection terminator
   - [x] Trailing comma in array
   - [x] Trailing comma in object
   - [x] Unquoted key
   - [x] Single quoted string
   - [x] Bad escape sequence
   - [x] Truncated string
   - [x] Extra data after document
   - [x] Missing colon
   - [x] Leading zero number
   - [x] NaN value
   - [x] Unclosed object
   - [x] Raw control character
- Problematic JSON
   - [x] Duplicate keys
   - [x] Large integer
   - [x] High precision decimal
   - [x] Exponent number
   - [x] Null vs missing field
   - [x] Escaped Unicode
   - [x] Empty object
   - [x] Top-level string
   - [x] Top-level number
   - [x] Top-level boolean
   - [x] Top-level null
   - [x] Empty body with JSON content type
   - [x] UTF-8 BOM prefix
   - [x] NDJSON
- API Returns malformed XML
   - [x] Missing final root element closure
   - [x] Mismatched tag
   - [x] Unescaped ampersand
   - [x] Duplicate attribute
   - [x] Undefined entity
   - [x] Multiple root elements
   - [x] Missing attribute quote
   - [x] Unclosed CDATA
   - [x] Invalid character
   - [x] Truncated document
   - [x] Bad processing instruction
- Problematic XML
   - [x] Attributes vs elements
   - [x] Empty vs missing vs nil
   - [x] Whitespace significant text
   - [x] CDATA content
   - [x] Mixed content
   - [x] Processing instruction
   - [x] Comments
   - [x] Encoding declaration mismatch
   - [x] DOCTYPE and DTD
   - [x] Empty body with XML content type
   - [x] UTF-8 BOM prefix
- API Returns mismatched content-type e.g. content-type xml, but is json
   - [x] json-xml
   - [x] xml-json
- Status code semantic mismatches
   - [x] 201 with no Location header
   - [x] 3xx with no redirect Location
   - [x] 204 with content
   - [x] 205 with content
   - [x] 304 with content
   - [x] 206 without Content-Range
   - [x] 401 without WWW-Authenticate
   - [x] 405 without Allow
   - [x] 416 without Content-Range
   - [x] 200 with error body
   - [x] 500 with success body

endpoints.label: Version - Missing Content-Type Header
endpoints.documentation: Returns a small JSON-looking version payload.
endpoints.problem: No content type header is explicitly set for the response.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should still show the raw body and make the missing or defaulted content type obvious.

endpoints.label: Good JSON - Valid JSON And Content-Type
endpoints.documentation: Returns valid JSON with a matching application/json content type.
endpoints.problem: Nothing is wrong with this response. It is the control sample.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should format or inspect this as valid JSON.

endpoints.label: Good XML - Valid XML And Content-Type
endpoints.documentation: Returns valid XML with a matching application/xml content type.
endpoints.problem: Nothing is wrong with this response. It is the XML control sample.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should format or inspect this as valid XML.

endpoints.label: Good Text - Plain Text And Content-Type
endpoints.documentation: Returns plain text with a matching text/plain content type.
endpoints.problem: Nothing is wrong with this response. It is the plain text control sample.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should show the response as readable text and make the content type visible.
endpoints.body:
API From Hell plain text response.
Line two keeps this as normal text.

endpoints.label: Good HTML - Valid HTML And Content-Type
endpoints.documentation: Returns simple HTML with a matching text/html content type.
endpoints.problem: Nothing is wrong with this response. It is the HTML control sample.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should make it clear whether it is showing rendered HTML or raw HTML.
endpoints.body:
HTML response

This is valid HTML.

endpoints.label: Good CSV - Valid CSV And Content-Type
endpoints.documentation: Returns small comma-separated data with a matching text/csv content type.
endpoints.problem: Nothing is wrong with this response. It is the CSV control sample.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should show the raw CSV and, if supported, provide a readable table view.

endpoints.label: Good YAML - Valid YAML And Content-Type
endpoints.documentation: Returns a small YAML document with a matching application/yaml content type.
endpoints.problem: Nothing is wrong with this response. It is the YAML control sample.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should preserve indentation and make the YAML media type visible.
endpoints.body:
name: API From Hell
items:
  - id: 1
    active: true
  - id: 2
    active: false

endpoints.label: Good Form URL Encoded - Valid Form Body And Content-Type
endpoints.documentation: Returns URL-encoded key/value data with a matching application/x-www-form-urlencoded content type.
endpoints.problem: Nothing is wrong with this response. It is the form-urlencoded control sample.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should show the raw encoded body and ideally decode the key/value pairs clearly.

endpoints.label: Good Octet Stream - Binary-Looking Body And Content-Type
endpoints.documentation: Returns a small binary-looking payload with an application/octet-stream content type.
endpoints.problem: Nothing is wrong with this response. It is the octet-stream control sample.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should make it clear whether it is showing bytes, text, or offering a download.

endpoints.label: Missing Content-Type - JSON Body
endpoints.documentation: Returns a JSON body without declaring a Content-Type header.
endpoints.problem: The body looks like JSON but the server does not declare application/json.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should show that the content type is missing and avoid hiding how it inferred the format.

endpoints.label: Missing Content-Type - XML Body
endpoints.documentation: Returns an XML body without declaring a Content-Type header.
endpoints.problem: The body looks like XML but the server does not declare application/xml.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should show that the content type is missing and avoid hiding how it inferred the format.

endpoints.label: Missing Content-Type - HTML Body
endpoints.documentation: Returns an HTML body without declaring a Content-Type header.
endpoints.problem: The body looks like HTML but the server does not declare text/html.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should show that the content type is missing and make rendered-vs-raw behavior obvious.

endpoints.label: Malformed JSON - Missing Collection Terminator
endpoints.documentation: Returns a JSON array that starts correctly but never closes the collection.
endpoints.problem: The body is malformed JSON even though the status is 200 and content type says application/json.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should show a parse/formatting failure and still make the raw body available.

endpoints.label: Malformed JSON - Trailing Comma In Array
endpoints.documentation: Returns a JSON array with a trailing comma before the closing bracket.
endpoints.problem: JSON does not allow a trailing comma after the final array item.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should show that JSON parsing failed and still make the raw body available.

endpoints.label: Malformed JSON - Trailing Comma In Object
endpoints.documentation: Returns a JSON object with a trailing comma before the closing brace.
endpoints.problem: JSON does not allow a trailing comma after the final object property.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should flag the parse failure near the end of the object.

endpoints.label: Malformed JSON - Unquoted Object Key
endpoints.documentation: Returns an object where the property name is not wrapped in double quotes.
endpoints.problem: JSON object keys must be double-quoted strings.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should not treat JavaScript object literal syntax as valid JSON.

endpoints.label: Malformed JSON - Single Quoted String
endpoints.documentation: Returns JSON-looking data using single quotes for strings.
endpoints.problem: JSON strings must use double quotes, not single quotes.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should distinguish JSON from JavaScript-like object notation.

endpoints.label: Malformed JSON - Bad Escape Sequence
endpoints.documentation: Returns a string with an unsupported escape sequence.
endpoints.problem: JSON allows only specific escape sequences; \q is not valid.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should show where the bad escape sequence breaks parsing.

endpoints.label: Malformed JSON - Truncated String
endpoints.documentation: Returns an object with a string value that never closes.
endpoints.problem: The response ends before the string terminator is present.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should make it clear that the body was incomplete or truncated.

endpoints.label: Malformed JSON - Extra Data After Document
endpoints.documentation: Returns one complete JSON object followed by another value.
endpoints.problem: A JSON response body should contain one complete JSON document, not extra data after it.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should show that the first object parsed but unexpected data follows.

endpoints.label: Malformed JSON - Missing Colon
endpoints.documentation: Returns an object property without the colon between key and value.
endpoints.problem: JSON object members require a colon between the name and value.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should identify the structural syntax error.

endpoints.label: Malformed JSON - Leading Zero Number
endpoints.documentation: Returns a numeric value with a leading zero.
endpoints.problem: JSON numbers cannot use leading zeroes like 01.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should reject the number rather than silently accepting non-standard JSON.

endpoints.label: Malformed JSON - NaN Value
endpoints.documentation: Returns NaN as a value in a JSON-looking object.
endpoints.problem: NaN is not a valid JSON number or literal.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should not silently accept JavaScript-only numeric values as JSON.

endpoints.label: Malformed JSON - Unclosed Object
endpoints.documentation: Returns an object that never closes with a final brace.
endpoints.problem: The JSON object starts correctly but the closing brace is missing.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should report the incomplete object and preserve the raw response.

endpoints.label: Malformed JSON - Raw Control Character
endpoints.documentation: Returns a string containing a raw control character.
endpoints.problem: JSON strings cannot contain unescaped control characters.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should show that the body is not valid JSON and still allow raw inspection.

endpoints.label: Problematic JSON - Duplicate Keys
endpoints.documentation: Returns valid JSON syntax with the same object key repeated.
endpoints.problem: Duplicate object keys are ambiguous because parsers disagree on whether the first or last value wins.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should make duplicate keys visible or warn that values may be overwritten.

endpoints.label: Problematic JSON - Large Integer
endpoints.documentation: Returns an integer larger than JavaScript can safely represent exactly.
endpoints.problem: Large JSON numbers can lose precision in clients that store all numbers as floating point values.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should display the exact raw value and avoid silently rounding it.

endpoints.label: Problematic JSON - High Precision Decimal
endpoints.documentation: Returns a decimal value with more precision than many clients preserve.
endpoints.problem: Some parsers or viewers round high precision decimal values when rendering or generating code.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should make the raw numeric text available so precision loss can be spotted.

endpoints.label: Problematic JSON - Exponent Number
endpoints.documentation: Returns JSON numbers using exponent notation.
endpoints.problem: Exponent notation is valid JSON, but some clients display, sort, or generate examples awkwardly.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should show both the raw exponent form and any interpreted value clearly.

endpoints.label: Problematic JSON - Null Vs Missing Field
endpoints.documentation: Returns one item with a null field and another item where the same field is missing.
endpoints.problem: Null and missing fields are semantically different but many clients and generated models blur them.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should make it easy to see that one value is explicit null and another is absent.

endpoints.label: Problematic JSON - Escaped Unicode
endpoints.documentation: Returns escaped Unicode including a zero-width space and a visually similar character.
endpoints.problem: Escaped Unicode can make values look normal while containing hidden or confusable characters.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should let users inspect raw escapes as well as rendered text.

endpoints.label: Problematic JSON - Empty Object
endpoints.documentation: Returns an empty JSON object where useful data would usually be expected.
endpoints.problem: An empty object is valid JSON, but it may violate the semantic contract a client expects.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should make it clear that the response is valid but contains no fields.

endpoints.label: Problematic JSON - Top-Level String
endpoints.documentation: Returns a top-level JSON string instead of an object or array.
endpoints.problem: Top-level strings are valid JSON but many clients and schemas assume an object or array.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should show that the complete JSON document is a scalar string.

endpoints.label: Problematic JSON - Top-Level Number
endpoints.documentation: Returns a top-level JSON number instead of an object or array.
endpoints.problem: Top-level numbers are valid JSON but can surprise clients that expect structured data.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should show that the entire response body is one scalar number.

endpoints.label: Problematic JSON - Top-Level Boolean
endpoints.documentation: Returns a top-level JSON boolean.
endpoints.problem: Top-level booleans are valid JSON but can expose assumptions in viewers and generated clients.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should display the boolean as the whole document, not as missing body content.

endpoints.label: Problematic JSON - Top-Level Null
endpoints.documentation: Returns a top-level JSON null value.
endpoints.problem: Top-level null is valid JSON but can be confused with an empty response or missing data.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should distinguish the literal null body from no body.

endpoints.label: Problematic JSON - Empty Body With JSON Content-Type
endpoints.documentation: Returns application/json with an empty response body.
endpoints.problem: An empty body is not a valid JSON document, even when the content type says JSON.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should show the empty body and not pretend it parsed a JSON value.

endpoints.label: Problematic JSON - UTF-8 BOM Prefix
endpoints.documentation: Returns a JSON object prefixed with a UTF-8 byte order mark.
endpoints.problem: Some JSON parsers tolerate a BOM and others reject or expose it as an unexpected character.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should preserve enough raw detail to diagnose the prefix if parsing fails.

endpoints.label: Problematic JSON - NDJSON Stream
endpoints.documentation: Returns newline-delimited JSON objects rather than a single JSON document.
endpoints.problem: NDJSON is useful as a stream format, but it is not one JSON document and needs different handling.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should show this as line-delimited data rather than trying to parse it as one object.

endpoints.label: Malformed XML - Missing Root Terminator
endpoints.documentation: Returns XML that starts correctly but does not close the document.
endpoints.problem: The body is malformed XML even though the status is 200 and content type says application/xml.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should show a parse/formatting failure and still make the raw body available.

endpoints.label: Malformed XML - Mismatched Tag
endpoints.documentation: Returns XML where a child element closes with the wrong tag name.
endpoints.problem: The item element opens as item but closes as thing.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should show the mismatched closing tag error and raw XML.

endpoints.label: Malformed XML - Unescaped Ampersand
endpoints.documentation: Returns XML containing an ampersand that is not escaped as an entity.
endpoints.problem: Ampersands in text content must be escaped as & unless they start a valid entity.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should show the entity parsing error and raw response text.

endpoints.label: Malformed XML - Duplicate Attribute
endpoints.documentation: Returns XML with the same attribute declared twice on one element.
endpoints.problem: An XML element cannot have two attributes with the same name.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should show the duplicate attribute parse error.

endpoints.label: Malformed XML - Undefined Entity
endpoints.documentation: Returns XML that references an entity which has not been declared.
endpoints.problem: The &unknown; entity is not predefined or declared in the document.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should expose the undefined entity error.

endpoints.label: Malformed XML - Multiple Root Elements
endpoints.documentation: Returns two top-level elements in one XML document.
endpoints.problem: A well-formed XML document must have exactly one root element.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should show that content appears after the first complete root.

endpoints.label: Malformed XML - Missing Attribute Quote
endpoints.documentation: Returns XML where an attribute value is not quoted.
endpoints.problem: XML attribute values must be wrapped in quotes.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should identify the attribute syntax error.

endpoints.label: Malformed XML - Unclosed CDATA
endpoints.documentation: Returns XML with a CDATA section that never terminates.
endpoints.problem: CDATA sections must close with ]]>. This response never closes the section.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should report the unterminated CDATA section.

endpoints.label: Malformed XML - Invalid Character
endpoints.documentation: Returns XML containing a character that is not allowed in XML 1.0 content.
endpoints.problem: The body includes a raw control character inside the text node.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should show the invalid character problem without losing the raw response.

endpoints.label: Malformed XML - Truncated Document
endpoints.documentation: Returns XML that starts a nested element but ends before closing it.
endpoints.problem: The document is incomplete and ends before the open elements are closed.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should report the unexpected end of document.

endpoints.label: Malformed XML - Bad Processing Instruction
endpoints.documentation: Returns XML with a processing instruction that never closes.
endpoints.problem: Processing instructions must close with ?> before normal element markup continues.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should show the processing instruction syntax error.

endpoints.label: Problematic XML - Attributes Vs Elements
endpoints.documentation: Returns the same kind of data in both attributes and child elements.
endpoints.problem: Clients often flatten XML into maps and may hide whether a value came from an attribute or an element.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should make attributes and elements visibly distinct and avoid silently merging conflicting values.

endpoints.label: Problematic XML - Empty Vs Missing Vs Nil
endpoints.documentation: Returns empty, missing, and explicitly nil values in one document.
endpoints.problem: Empty elements, absent elements, and xsi:nil values are different states but clients may render them identically.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should help users distinguish empty, missing, and nil values.

endpoints.label: Problematic XML - Whitespace Significant Text
endpoints.documentation: Returns XML text values with leading, trailing, and multiline whitespace.
endpoints.problem: Pretty printers and object mappers can trim or normalize whitespace that is part of the value.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should let users inspect the raw text and spot whitespace-preservation issues.
endpoints.body:
<?xml version="1.0"?><root><value>  padded value  </value><multiline>
  line one
  line two
</multiline></root>

endpoints.label: Problematic XML - CDATA Content
endpoints.documentation: Returns XML with JSON-looking and HTML-looking text inside CDATA.
endpoints.problem: Clients may hide CDATA boundaries or try to interpret embedded markup as real XML.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should preserve CDATA content as text and make the raw body available.

endpoints.label: Problematic XML - Mixed Content
endpoints.documentation: Returns text and child elements mixed inside the same element.
endpoints.problem: Mixed content is valid XML but awkward for clients that convert XML to simple JSON-like objects.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should show text nodes and child elements without dropping their order.

endpoints.label: Problematic XML - Processing Instruction
endpoints.documentation: Returns a valid processing instruction before the document root.
endpoints.problem: Processing instructions are valid XML but many viewers hide or ignore them.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should make processing instructions visible in raw or structured views.

endpoints.label: Problematic XML - Comments
endpoints.documentation: Returns XML containing comments between normal elements.
endpoints.problem: Comments may be hidden by formatters or stripped by object conversion.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should preserve enough raw detail to show whether comments were present.

endpoints.label: Problematic XML - Encoding Declaration Mismatch
endpoints.documentation: Returns XML where the HTTP charset and XML declaration disagree.
endpoints.problem: The response header says UTF-8 but the XML declaration says ISO-8859-1.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should expose both the HTTP charset and XML declaration so encoding assumptions are visible.

endpoints.label: Problematic XML - DOCTYPE And DTD
endpoints.documentation: Returns XML with a safe inline DTD declaration.
endpoints.problem: DOCTYPE and DTD declarations are valid XML but clients may block, ignore, or hide them for security reasons.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should show that the document includes a DTD without attempting unsafe external resolution.
endpoints.body: <?xml version="1.0"?><!DOCTYPE note [<!ELEMENT note (to,body)><!ELEMENT to (#PCDATA)><!ELEMENT body (#PCDATA)>]><note><to>client</to><body>inline doctype only</body></note>

endpoints.label: Problematic XML - Empty Body With XML Content-Type
endpoints.documentation: Returns application/xml with an empty response body.
endpoints.problem: An empty body is not an XML document, even when the content type says XML.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should show the empty body and not pretend it parsed XML.

endpoints.label: Problematic XML - UTF-8 BOM Prefix
endpoints.documentation: Returns XML prefixed with a UTF-8 byte order mark.
endpoints.problem: Some clients hide the BOM while others expose it as a leading character or parsing issue.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should preserve enough raw detail to diagnose the prefix if parsing behaves unexpectedly.

endpoints.label: Content-Type Mismatch - Header JSON Body XML
endpoints.documentation: Returns XML while declaring application/json.
endpoints.problem: The Content-Type header is application/json but the body is XML.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should make the mismatch visible and allow you to inspect the raw response.

endpoints.label: Content-Type Mismatch - Header XML Body JSON
endpoints.documentation: Returns JSON while declaring application/xml.
endpoints.problem: The Content-Type header is application/xml but the body is JSON.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should make the mismatch visible and allow you to inspect the raw response.

endpoints.label: 201 Missing Location Header
endpoints.documentation: Returns a created-looking JSON response.
endpoints.problem: The status is 201 Created, but the response does not include a Location header for the created resource.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should make the missing Location header easy to spot.

endpoints.label: 301 Missing Location Header
endpoints.documentation: Returns a permanent redirect status.
endpoints.problem: The status is 301 Moved Permanently, but there is no Location header to follow.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should show that the redirect target is missing.
endpoints.body: Moved permanently, but no Location header was supplied.

endpoints.label: 302 Missing Location Header
endpoints.documentation: Returns a common temporary redirect status.
endpoints.problem: The status is 302 Found, but there is no Location header to follow.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should show that the redirect target is missing.
endpoints.body: Found somewhere else, but no Location header was supplied.

endpoints.label: 307 Missing Location Header
endpoints.documentation: Returns a temporary redirect status that should preserve the original method.
endpoints.problem: The status is 307 Temporary Redirect, but there is no Location header to follow.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should show that the redirect target is missing and not silently change method.
endpoints.body: Temporary redirect, but no Location header was supplied.

endpoints.label: 204 Body Present Despite No Content
endpoints.documentation: Returns 204 No Content while the catalog defines a JSON response body.
endpoints.problem: The status says no content, but the endpoint attempts to send response content.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should make it clear whether the body is present, discarded, or hidden by the HTTP stack.
endpoints.body: {"message":"This body should not be sent with 204 No Content"}

endpoints.label: 205 Body Present Despite Reset Content
endpoints.documentation: Returns 205 Reset Content while the catalog defines a JSON response body.
endpoints.problem: The status tells the client to reset the document view and should not include a response body.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should make the body/status contradiction visible.
endpoints.body: {"message":"This body should not be sent with 205 Reset Content"}

endpoints.label: 304 Body Present Despite Not Modified
endpoints.documentation: Returns 304 Not Modified while the catalog defines a JSON response body.
endpoints.problem: The status is a cache validation response and should not include a response body.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should make it clear whether the body is present, discarded, or hidden by cache/status handling.
endpoints.body: {"message":"This body should not be sent with 304 Not Modified"}

endpoints.label: 206 Missing Content-Range Header
endpoints.documentation: Returns a partial-looking body.
endpoints.problem: The status is 206 Partial Content, but the response does not include Content-Range.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should make the missing range metadata easy to spot.

endpoints.label: 401 Missing WWW-Authenticate Header
endpoints.documentation: Returns an authentication failure body.
endpoints.problem: The status is 401 Unauthorized, but the response does not include WWW-Authenticate.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should show the missing authentication challenge header.
endpoints.body: {"error":"authentication required but no challenge header was sent"}

endpoints.label: 405 Missing Allow Header
endpoints.documentation: Returns a method-not-allowed response body.
endpoints.problem: The status is 405 Method Not Allowed, but the response does not include Allow.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should show the missing allowed-methods metadata.
endpoints.body: {"error":"method not allowed but no Allow header was sent"}

endpoints.label: 416 Missing Content-Range Header
endpoints.documentation: Returns an unsatisfied range response body.
endpoints.problem: The status is 416 Range Not Satisfiable, but the response does not include Content-Range.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should show the missing range explanation.
endpoints.body: {"error":"range not satisfiable but no Content-Range header was sent"}

endpoints.label: 200 Error-Shaped Body
endpoints.documentation: Returns a normal success status.
endpoints.problem: The status is 200 OK, but the body is shaped like an error response.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should make it easy to inspect both status and body without assuming the request succeeded.

endpoints.label: 500 Success-Shaped Body
endpoints.documentation: Returns a server error status.
endpoints.problem: The status is 500 Internal Server Error, but the body claims success.
endpoints.expectation: A REST client should make it easy to inspect both status and body without assuming the body is truthful.
endpoints.body: {"success":true,"message":"The body says success but the status is 500"}
